programs.programName: TennCare Medicare Savings Programs (QMB, SLMB, QI)

verified.notes: Official TN.gov source lists annual resource limits ($9,950 individual, $14,910 couple) for QMB/SLMB/QI; no 2026 monthly income limits found in results. Federal MSP limits for 2026 in medicare.gov not specified numerically.

programs.programName: Tennessee State Health Insurance Assistance Program (TN SHIP)

verified.notes: TN SHIP is a counseling and assistance program for Medicare-eligible individuals, not an eligibility-based benefit program with age, income, or asset limits. The program provides free Medicare counseling to all Medicare beneficiaries and their families/caregivers. No minimum age requirement, income test, or asset test applies to receive SHIP counseling services. SHIP assists beneficiaries in applying for separate programs (Medicare Savings Plans, Extra Help) that do have income limits, but SHIP itself has no eligibility restrictions beyond Medicare eligibility.

verified.notes: Tennessee Aging Nutrition Program is the official statewide program. Age 60+ is the senior-specific threshold across all regional programs (Meals on Wheels Northeast Tennessee, Mid-Cumberland, Nashville Metro Social Services). No income or asset limits found in official sources. Regional programs may have additional eligibility criteria; contact local Area Agency on Aging for details. Phone number is the official state hotline for all 95 Tennessee counties.

verified.notes: Program serves seniors age 60+ as primary population for NFCSP senior pathway. Income test exists (185% Federal Poverty Guidelines triggers contribution encouragement) but specific dollar thresholds not provided in retrieved sources. No asset limits found in authoritative sources. Program active as of April 2026.

verified.notes: Income limit based on 125% of federal poverty level (2026 figures: $1,083/month for individual, $1,458/month for couple at 100% FPL). Phone number is East Tennessee Human Resources regional coordinator; additional regional coordinators available via TN.gov source. Program remains active as of April 2026.

programs.notes: Income limit based on 125% of federal poverty level (2026 figures: $1,083/month for individual, $1,458/month for couple at 100% FPL). Phone number is East Tennessee Human Resources regional coordinator; additional regional coordinators available via TN.gov source. Program remains active as of April 2026.

programs.programName: Tennessee Senior Law Alliance (TSLA) / Tennessee Senior Legal Helpline

verified.notes: Program explicitly serves seniors aged 60+. No income or asset limits found in available sources; helpline states services available 'regardless of your income or assets.' TSLA is active as of 2026 and operates statewide across all 95 Tennessee counties. Phone number is for Free Senior Legal Helpline (844-HELP4TN).

verified.notes: Tennessee Long-Term Care Ombudsman Program is an advocacy service, not a benefits program with eligibility criteria. No age, income, or asset limits apply. The program serves any resident of a long-term care facility (nursing homes, assisted living, homes for the aged, adult care homes). Phone number is for the State Long-Term Care Ombudsman office in Nashville. Regional district ombudsmen also available for specific counties.

verified.notes: OPTIONS for Community Living serves adults age 60+ (elderly) and adults with physical disabilities age 18+. Senior-specific minimum age is 60. Program has no income eligibility requirement but uses sliding fee scale based on income. No asset limits found in official sources. Applicants must meet ADL/IADL limitation requirements.
